summary: Great NYE matrix recording with clean mix and standout performances (Let‑The‑Good‑Times‑Roll, The Wheel, Franklin’s Tower) despite occasional bass clipping.
review: Fans rave about the high‑energy New Year’s Eve show – the saxophone cameo by Clarence Clemons, a monster Drums segment and soaring jams on Let‑The‑Good‑Times‑Roll, The Wheel and Franklin’s Tower.  The matrix source delivers a well‑balanced SBD/audience blend that sounds clean overall, though several reviewers note an over‑driven bass that clips in places.

ai_rating.rationale: 15 reviews with a mean rating of ~4.27; overwhelmingly positive comments about performance and sound, only isolated complaints about bass clipping.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix of soundboard and audience; many praise the clean sound. A few note an exaggerated/clip‑prone Phil bass (possible pre‑FM feed) that detracts slightly from perfection.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a rolling Let‑The‑Good‑Times‑Roll, builds through tight first‑song energy and maintains momentum with strong set selections; seamless transitions (e.g., Wheel into Gimme) keep the NYE vibe high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crisp guitar tone, extra umph on Franklins, compelling vocal on “Inspiration”, and engaging stage presence.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is front‑and‑center – many love the heavy mix, though a few report clipping/over‑drive that muddies the low end.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ work shines (lights up LTGTR, ethereal intro to GSL) and adds vigor throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Hailed as one of the best ever; guests like Olatunji and Kitaro add texture, and the drum/space segment is a highlight.
